summary: Tight second set with soaring Comes A Time and fiery Scarlet>Fire jam
blurb: The show shines in the second set, delivering a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Estimated Prophet → Comes A Time flow and a powerful Miracle jam. Jerry’s lead work on Comes A Time is praised, while Phil’s bass anchors Jack Straw. Overall performance is solid despite mixed opinions on recording quality.
review: December 27, 1990 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum captured the Dead in a vibrant late‑90s mood. The first set offered solid staples like Jack Straw and Loose Lucy, but it was the second set that truly lifted the night – a flawless segue from Scarlet Begonias into Fire On The Mountain, then Estimated Prophet and an emotionally charged Comes A Time that had Jerry’s “crying” guitar soaring over the band. That momentum carried straight into an inspired Miracle jam, with Mickey’s chanting during drums adding a memorable texture. Phil’s bass was described as both a “bomb” on Jack Straw and a big, clear presence in audience recordings, anchoring the groove throughout. While keyboards from Bruce Hornsby and Vince Welnick received mixed remarks and didn’t dominate the night, the overall playing felt tight and polished, making this show a solid entry for fans of the era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings (soundboard and audience) are clearly positive, highlighting Comes A Time, the Scarlet>Fire sequence, and a tight Miracle jam. One mixed review notes a floppy feel but offers no strong criticism of the playing. A single negative rating provides no detailed musical complaints. The preponderance of praise yields a moderate positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture with clear stereo, minimal drop‑outs, and it showcases the standout songs and tight second‑set flow.

key_highlights: Seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Estimated Prophet → Comes A Time sequence
Jerry’s soaring vocal and guitar on Comes A Time
Tight, energetic Miracle jam in the second set
Strong Phil bass presence noted especially in front‑of‑house audience recordings

band_performance.Jerry: Delivered a ‘crying’ guitar tone on Comes A Time and led the band with stellar lead work, including an unforgettable vocal entry.
band_performance.Phil: Provided a powerful low‑end foundation; his bass was described as a ‘bomb’ on Jack Straw and highlighted for its clarity in audience recordings.
band_performance.Vince Welnick: Keyboard contributions received mixed remarks, with no specific highlights noted for the night.
band_performance.Mickey: His drum chanting during the set was singled out as a memorable, beautiful moment.
